findings.title: Redundant comment restates obvious loop-exit condition
findings.description: The comment 'If this chunk reached the end of text, stop' directly restates what the code does. The condition `end >= text.length` followed by `break` is self-explanatory. This comment adds no information beyond what the code already communicates and has a small negative ROI due to maintenance cost if the termination logic changes.
findings.suggestion: Remove the comment. The code is self-documenting: `if (end >= text.length) break` clearly conveys the exit condition.

findings.title: Test inline comment restates the next line of code
findings.code_snippet:
// Create a minimal valid PDF with embedded text "Hello PDF"
const pdfContent = createMinimalPdf('Hello PDF')
findings.description: The comment 'Create a minimal valid PDF with embedded text "Hello PDF"' restates what the function call `createMinimalPdf('Hello PDF')` already communicates. The function name and argument are self-documenting. In test files, step-narration comments can be helpful, but this one adds no information beyond what the code shows.
findings.suggestion: Remove the comment or consolidate test steps into a higher-level comment describing the test's intent rather than restating each line.

positive_observations: All JSDoc comments on exported classes and functions are factually accurate -- parameter descriptions match actual parameters, return type descriptions match actual returns, and method behavior descriptions match implementation.

The 'Imperative Shell' and 'Pure function' architecture annotations on class/function JSDoc correctly identify which layer each module belongs to, which will help future maintainers understand the codebase's architecture.
The comment explaining pdfjs-dist legacy build import (line 51 of document-processor.ts) is an excellent 'why' comment that explains a non-obvious technical decision.

The OOXML namespace comments in the test file explain both what the constants are (ISO/IEC 29500 URIs) and why they are built via concatenation (lint false-positive avoidance) -- high-value explanatory comments.

The Test Double rationale comment in document-pipeline.integration.test.ts follows the project convention from CLAUDE.md and clearly explains why a deterministic embedder is used instead of a real API.
The @parcel/watcher timing behavior comment in folder-monitor.test.ts (line 98) prevents future developers from incorrectly tightening the assertion to expect only 'update' events.
No forbidden patterns (TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX, PLACEHOLDER, TEMP) found in any of the 7 reviewed files.
